violations.detail: Back contains ', avoiding' — a participle joining two facts: (1) stack memory is contiguous, (2) this avoids fragmentation overhead. Must split into two cards.
Back contains '—' dash joining two facts: (1) lifetime is not tied to scope, (2) they persist until explicitly freed. Must split into two cards.
Back contains ', requiring' — a participle joining two facts: (1) size is unknown at compile time, (2) runtime allocation is required. Must split into two cards.
Card 1 ('Why is stack faster than heap?') and Card 4 ('Why is heap slower than stack?') are the same question from opposite directions. Knowing one trivially gives the other. Must merge or drop one.
Two independent facts packed into one cloze: c1=malloc/new (allocation API) and c2=free/delete (deallocation API). Each fill is an independent fact — must split into separate cards.

violations.rule: card selection — specific constant value without understanding
violations.detail: Asks for a specific numeric value ('1–8 MB') without testing why that value matters or what consequences it has. Falls under 'specific constant values without understanding why they matter' anti-pattern.
